dhp221:1: Abandon anger, be done with conceit,
dhp221:3: When for name & form you have no attachment
dhp221:4: –have nothing at all– no sufferings, no stresses, invade.
dhp222:2: whoever keeps firm control as if with a racing chariot:
dhp222:3: him I call a master charioteer.
dhp222:4: Anyone else, a rein-holder– that’s all.
dhp223:1: Conquer anger with lack of anger;
dhp224:1:
By telling the truth;
by not growing angry;
dhp224:2: by giving, when asked, no matter how little you have:
dhp224:4: you enter the presence of devas.
dhp225:4: where, having gone, there’s no grief.
dhp226:4: their effluents come to an end.
dhp227:1: This has come down from old, Atula,
dhp227:3: they find fault with one who sits silent,
dhp227:4: they find fault with one who speaks a great deal,
dhp227:5: they find fault with one who measures his words.
dhp227:6: There’s no one unfaulted in the world.
dhp229:2: having observed him day after day
dhp229:3: to be blameless in conduct, intelligent,
dhp229:4:
endowed with discernment & virtue:
like an ingot of gold– who’s fit to find fault with him?
Even devas praise him.
Even by Brahma he’s praised.
dhp231:1: Guard against anger erupting in body;
dhp231:4: live conducting yourself well in body.
dhp232:1: Guard against anger erupting in speech;
dhp232:4: live conducting yourself well in speech.
dhp233:1: Guard against anger erupting in mind;
dhp233:4: live conducting yourself well in mind.
dhp234:4: are the ones whose restraint is secure.
